audience: Grade 5 (ages 10-11) — a gentle introduction to the National Science Bowl format and topics
style_guide: Simple, friendly wording. One clear idea per question. Mix of multiple-choice (W/X/Y/Z) and short-answer, across the four NSB topic areas. Facts kept at an elementary level.

tossup_variants.question: What gas do humans need to breathe in from the air to stay alive?

bonus_variants.question: Which of these is NOT a living thing? W) a tree X) a dog Y) a rock Z) a fish

tossup_variants.question: Which part of a plant takes in water and nutrients from the soil? W) leaves X) flower Y) roots Z) petals

bonus_variants.question: Plants make their own food using sunlight. What gas do they give off that animals need to breathe?

tossup_variants.question: What do we call an animal that eats only plants?

bonus_variants.question: Which of these animals is a mammal? W) frog X) shark Y) snake Z) dolphin

tossup_variants.question: Which organ pumps blood all around your body? W) lungs X) brain Y) heart Z) stomach

bonus_variants.question: How many bones are in a typical adult human body?

tossup_variants.question: Before it becomes a butterfly, what is the young, wormlike stage called?

bonus_variants.question: Which stage comes FIRST in a frog's life cycle? W) frog X) tadpole Y) egg Z) froglet

tossup_variants.question: In a food chain, what do we call an animal that hunts and eats other animals? W) producer X) predator Y) prey Z) decomposer

bonus_variants.question: What is the name of the green substance in leaves that helps a plant make food?

tossup_variants.question: Which of these is a gas? W) ice X) liquid water Y) steam Z) a rock

bonus_variants.question: When liquid water gets very cold and freezes, what solid does it turn into?

tossup_variants.question: A magnet strongly attracts objects made of which common metal?

bonus_variants.question: Which object would a magnet stick to? W) a plastic spoon X) a steel paperclip Y) a wooden block Z) a glass cup

tossup_variants.question: What force pulls objects down toward the ground? W) magnetism X) friction Y) gravity Z) electricity

bonus_variants.question: What force slows down a ball as it rolls across the floor and finally stops it?

tossup_variants.question: What star is the main source of light and heat for the Earth?

bonus_variants.question: Which of these MAKES its own light? W) the Moon X) a mirror Y) a light bulb Z) a window

tossup_variants.question: Water is made of two elements joined together. Which two? W) hydrogen and oxygen X) carbon and oxygen Y) salt and sugar Z) iron and air

bonus_variants.question: What do we call water after it has turned into a gas?

tossup_variants.question: True or false: sound can travel through water.

bonus_variants.question: Which travels faster? W) light X) sound Y) they are exactly the same Z) neither one moves

tossup_variants.question: Which planet is closest to the Sun? W) Earth X) Mercury Y) Mars Z) Jupiter

bonus_variants.question: What do we call the path that a planet travels around the Sun?

tossup_variants.question: About how much of the Earth's surface is covered by water? W) about one quarter X) about one half Y) about three quarters Z) almost none

bonus_variants.question: What do we call the very center layer of the Earth?

tossup_variants.question: What is it called when water turns into vapor and rises into the air? W) condensation X) precipitation Y) evaporation Z) collection

bonus_variants.question: Rain, snow, sleet, and hail are all examples of what part of the water cycle? (one word for water falling from clouds)

tossup_variants.question: What instrument do we use to measure temperature?

bonus_variants.question: What are clouds mostly made of? W) smoke X) tiny water droplets Y) cotton Z) dust

tossup_variants.question: What is the name of Earth's only natural satellite?

bonus_variants.question: What do we call the Moon when it looks like a thin curved sliver? W) full moon X) new moon Y) crescent moon Z) half moon

tossup_variants.question: Which of these is a fossil fuel that we burn for energy? W) wood X) coal Y) water Z) wind

bonus_variants.question: What word describes hot, melted rock AFTER it has erupted out of a volcano onto the surface?

tossup_variants.question: What is 7 multiplied by 8?

bonus_variants.question: What is 144 divided by 12?

tossup_variants.question: Which of these fractions is the LARGEST? W) one half X) one quarter Y) one third Z) one eighth

bonus_variants.question: What is one half plus one quarter? Give your answer as a fraction.

tossup_variants.question: How many sides does a hexagon have?

bonus_variants.question: What do we call a triangle that has all three sides the same length? W) right triangle X) scalene triangle Y) equilateral triangle Z) isosceles triangle

tossup_variants.question: How many minutes are there in 2 hours?

bonus_variants.question: A rectangle is 5 centimeters long and 4 centimeters wide. What is its area in square centimeters?

tossup_variants.question: What number comes next in this pattern: 2, 4, 6, 8, __ ? W) 9 X) 10 Y) 12 Z) 16

bonus_variants.question: In the number 4,725, what is the value of the digit 7?

tossup_variants.question: Round the number 48 to the nearest ten.

bonus_variants.question: If one pencil costs 25 cents, how many pencils can you buy with one dollar?
